Title: How to access your benefits summary — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How can a Engineering employee view their total benefits and compensation summary?

## Answer

Your Total Rewards Statement provides a comprehensive view of all compensation and benefits.

### Accessing Your Statement

1. Log in to ess.company.com
2. Navigate to "My Compensation" > "Total Rewards Statement"
3. Statement is updated quarterly (January, April, July, October)

### What's Included

| Component | Example |
|-----------|---------|
| Base Salary | $85,000 |
| Annual Bonus (target) | $8,500 (10%) |
| Equity/ESPP | $4,200 estimated value |
| Health Insurance (employer portion) | $9,600 |
| Dental & Vision (employer portion) | $1,200 |
| Life & Disability (employer portion) | $2,100 |
| 401(k) Match | $3,400 |
| HSA Contribution | $500 |
| Tuition Reimbursement Used | $5,250 |
| Gym Subsidy | $900 |
| **Total Compensation** | **$120,650** |

### Why It Matters

- Your base salary is only part of your total compensation
- Benefits typically add 30-40% to your base pay
- Use this during career planning and offer comparisons

### Sharing Your Statement

- PDF download available for personal records
- The statement is confidential — do not share with coworkers
- Useful for loan applications, financial planning, and tax preparation
Author: Learning & Development

Title: Fertility and family-building benefits — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What fertility and family-building benefits are available to Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company provides comprehensive fertility and family-building support through our medical plan.

### Coverage

| Treatment | Lifetime Maximum |
|-----------|-----------------|
| Diagnostic testing | Covered under medical plan |
| Fertility medications | $15,000 |
| IUI (Intrauterine Insemination) | $5,000 per cycle (up to 3 cycles) |
| IVF (In Vitro Fertilization) | $25,000 per cycle (up to 3 cycles) |
| Egg freezing | $10,000 (includes 1 year storage) |
| Egg/sperm donation | $10,000 |
| Surrogacy | $10,000 (see also Adoption & Surrogacy benefits) |
| Genetic testing (PGT) | $5,000 per cycle |
| **Total Lifetime Maximum** | **$75,000** |

### Eligibility

- All benefits-eligible employees and their covered spouses/partners
- No diagnosis of infertility required (available regardless of age, gender, marital status, or sexual orientation)
- Must use an in-network fertility specialist

### How to Access

1. Contact the fertility benefits navigator: 800-555-0321
2. Navigator helps you find in-network specialists and understand coverage
3. Pre-authorization required for IVF and egg freezing
4. File claims through your medical plan as usual

### Additional Support

- **Fertility Support Group**: Monthly virtual sessions led by a counselor
- **EAP Counseling**: 12 free sessions for fertility-related emotional support
- **Flexible Scheduling**: Work with your manager for appointment accommodations
- **Travel Benefit**: Up to $5,000 for travel if no in-network provider within 50 miles

### Leave

- Medical appointments: Use sick leave or flex time
- Recovery from procedures: Sick leave or STD if needed
- After birth/placement: 12-16 weeks paid parental leave
Author: Learning & Development

Title: Dependent care FSA details — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does the Dependent Care FSA work for Engineering employees?

## Answer

A Dependent Care FSA (DCFSA) lets you set aside pre-tax dollars for eligible dependent care expenses.

### Key Details

| Feature | Details |
|---------|---------|
| Annual maximum | $5,000 (married filing jointly or single) / $2,500 (married filing separately) |
| Eligibility | Full-time employees with eligible dependents |
| Enrollment | During open enrollment or within 30 days of qualifying life event |
| Funds availability | As contributed (not full amount on Day 1 like Healthcare FSA) |
| Use-it-or-lose-it | Yes — funds not used by March 15 of the following year are forfeited |
| Grace period | 2.5 months (through March 15) to incur expenses from prior year funds |

### Eligible Dependents

- Children under age 13
- Disabled spouse or dependent of any age who cannot care for themselves
- Dependent must live with you for more than half the year

### Eligible Expenses

- Daycare center or preschool
- Before/after school care programs
- Summer day camp
- In-home care (nanny, au pair, babysitter) for work-related care
- Elder daycare for a qualifying dependent

### Not Eligible

- Overnight camp
- Kindergarten or higher grade tuition
- Food, clothing, or entertainment
- Care provided by your spouse or child under 19
- Medical expenses (use Healthcare FSA instead)

### Tax Savings Example

Annual childcare cost: $10,000
DCFSA contribution: $5,000 (pre-tax)
Tax bracket: 30%
**Annual tax savings: $1,500**

### How to Use

1. Pay your dependent care provider
2. Submit a reimbursement claim via the benefits portal or app
3. Attach receipt showing: provider name, dates of service, dependent name, amount
4. Reimbursement deposited to your bank within 5-7 business days
5. Provider's Tax ID or SSN is required once per year
Author: Payroll Team

- Disabled spouse or dependent of any age who cannot care for themselves
- Dependent must live with you for more than half the year

### Eligible Expenses

- Daycare center or preschool
- Before/after school care programs
- Summer day camp
- In-home care (nanny, au pair, babysitter) for work-related care
- Elder daycare for a qualifying dependent

### Not Eligible

- Overnight camp
- Kindergarten or higher grade tuition
- Food, clothing, or entertainment
- Care provided by your spouse or child under 19
- Medical expenses (use Healthcare FSA instead)

### Tax Savings Example

Annual childcare cost: $10,000
DCFSA contribution: $5,000 (pre-tax)
Tax bracket: 30%
**Annual tax savings: $1,500**

### How to Use

1. Pay your dependent care provider
2. Submit a reimbursement claim via the benefits portal or app
3. Attach receipt showing: provider name, dates of service, dependent name, amount
4. Reimbursement deposited to your bank within 5-7 business days
5. Provider's Tax ID or SSN is required once per year
Author: Payroll Team

Title: Domestic violence leave policy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What leave and support are available to Engineering employees affected by domestic violence?

## Answer

The company provides confidential support and protected leave for employees experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking.

### Leave Entitlement

- Up to 10 days of paid Safe Leave per 12-month period
- Can be used for:
  - Medical treatment or counseling
  - Legal proceedings (restraining orders, court hearings)
  - Safety planning and relocation
  - Support services
- Additional unpaid leave available under FMLA if eligible

### How to Request

1. Notify HR confidentially: employeerelations@company.com or call ext. 4500
2. Advance notice preferred but not required (emergencies understood)
3. Documentation may be requested but is flexible:
   - Police report, restraining order, or court document
   - Letter from a domestic violence organization
   - Letter from a healthcare provider or counselor
   - Employee's own written statement

### Workplace Safety

- If there is a safety concern at work, notify Security immediately (ext. 5100)
- Safety accommodations available:
  - Workspace relocation
  - Changed phone number/email
  - Modified work schedule
  - Security escort to/from vehicle
  - Parking reassignment
- Photo of perpetrator provided to Security (with employee consent)

### Confidentiality

- All information is strictly confidential
- Only those with a need to know are informed (with your consent)
- HR maintains records in a separate, restricted file
- Your manager is told only what is necessary for scheduling

### Support Resources

| Resource | Contact |
|----------|---------|
| National DV Hotline | 800-799-7233 (24/7) |
| EAP Counseling | 800-555-0123 (free, confidential) |
| Local DV Shelter | Provided by EAP based on location |
| Legal Aid | Free 30-minute consult through EAP |

### Your Rights

- Protected from retaliation for requesting safe leave
- Protected from termination due to domestic violence situation
- Right to reasonable workplace accommodations
- Right to confidentiality
Author: Payroll Team

Author: HR Generalist

Title: Domestic violence leave policy — Operations department
Content:
## Question

What leave and support are available to Operations employees affected by domestic violence?

## Answer

The company provides confidential support and protected leave for employees experiencing domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking.

### Leave Entitlement

- Up to 10 days of paid Safe Leave per 12-month period
- Can be used for:
  - Medical treatment or counseling
  - Legal proceedings (restraining orders, court hearings)
  - Safety planning and relocation
  - Support services
- Additional unpaid leave available under FMLA if eligible

### How to Request

1. Notify HR confidentially: employeerelations@company.com or call ext. 4500
2. Advance notice preferred but not required (emergencies understood)
3. Documentation may be requested but is flexible:
   - Police report, restraining order, or court document
   - Letter from a domestic violence organization
   - Letter from a healthcare provider or counselor
   - Employee's own written statement

### Workplace Safety

- If there is a safety concern at work, notify Security immediately (ext. 5100)
- Safety accommodations available:
  - Workspace relocation
  - Changed phone number/email
  - Modified work schedule
  - Security escort to/from vehicle
  - Parking reassignment
- Photo of perpetrator provided to Security (with employee consent)

### Confidentiality

- All information is strictly confidential
- Only those with a need to know are informed (with your consent)
- HR maintains records in a separate, restricted file
- Your manager is told only what is necessary for scheduling

### Support Resources

| Resource | Contact |
|----------|---------|
| National DV Hotline | 800-799-7233 (24/7) |
| EAP Counseling | 800-555-0123 (free, confidential) |
| Local DV Shelter | Provided by EAP based on location |
| Legal Aid | Free 30-minute consult through EAP |

### Your Rights

- Protected from retaliation for requesting safe leave
- Protected from termination due to domestic violence situation
- Right to reasonable workplace accommodations
- Right to confidentiality

Author: Talent Acquisition

Title: Bone marrow and organ donation leave — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What leave is available for Engineering employees who are organ or bone marrow donors?

## Answer

The company supports employees who choose to donate organs or bone marrow by providing paid leave beyond standard sick leave.

### Leave Entitlement

| Donation Type | Paid Leave | Additional Unpaid |
|--------------|-----------|-------------------|
| Organ donation (kidney, liver lobe, etc.) | Up to 30 days | Up to 30 additional days |
| Bone marrow donation | Up to 5 days | Up to 5 additional days |
| Blood/platelet donation | Up to 4 hours per donation | N/A |

### How to Request

1. Notify your manager and HR as far in advance as possible
2. Submit a Medical Leave Request via the HR Portal
3. Provide a letter from the transplant center confirming:
   - You are an approved donor
   - Expected surgery/procedure date
   - Expected recovery period
4. HR coordinates the leave dates and return plan

### During Leave

- Full pay for the paid leave period
- All benefits continue
- PTO continues to accrue
- Job protection for the full leave period
- FMLA may run concurrently

### Blood and Platelet Donation

- Up to 4 hours of paid time for each donation (including travel)
- No limit on number of donations per year
- Coordinate timing with your manager
- Company hosts quarterly blood drives (on-site, during work hours)

### Recovery Support

- Gradual return-to-work schedule available
- Ergonomic accommodations if needed post-surgery
- EAP counseling for emotional support
- Follow-up medical appointments covered under sick leave

### Recognition

- Organ and bone marrow donors recognized in the company newsletter (with consent)
- Living Donor badge added to employee profile

Title: How to request an employment verification letter — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does a Engineering employee request employment verification for a lender, landlord, or other third party?

## Answer

Employment verification requests are handled by HR to ensure accuracy and privacy.

### Self-Service Verification (Automated)

For mortgage lenders, landlords, and background check companies:
- Direct them to our third-party verification service: **The Work Number** by Equifax
- Company code: **12345**
- Verifiers can access: employment dates, job title, and salary (with your consent)
- Available 24/7 at theworknumber.com
- No action needed from you (unless salary verification requires a salary key — see below)

### Salary Key (for income verification)

1. Log in to theworknumber.com/employees
2. Use company code 12345 and your SSN to log in
3. Generate a Salary Key
4. Share the Salary Key with the verifying party
5. Key expires after one use or 30 days

### Custom Verification Letters

If you need a signed letter on company letterhead (for immigration, visa, bank, etc.):
1. Submit a request at HR Portal > Forms > Employment Verification
2. Specify what information to include:
   - Employment dates
   - Current title and department
   - Salary (optional — your consent required)
   - Employment status (full-time, part-time)
   - Custom language (if specific wording is required)
3. HR processes the request within 3 business days
4. Letter sent via email (PDF) or mailed (upon request)

### What HR Will NOT Provide

- Performance evaluations or ratings
- Reason for separation (for former employees)
- Personal opinions or recommendations
- Information not authorized by the employee

### Manager Reference Calls

- Managers may provide professional references when authorized by the employee
- References should include only factual, job-related information
- HR recommends limiting verbal references to confirming employment dates and title
- For anything beyond basic verification, direct the caller to HR
Author: Learning & Development

- Verifiers can access: employment dates, job title, and salary (with your consent)
- Available 24/7 at theworknumber.com
- No action needed from you (unless salary verification requires a salary key — see below)

### Salary Key (for income verification)

1. Log in to theworknumber.com/employees
2. Use company code 12345 and your SSN to log in
3. Generate a Salary Key
4. Share the Salary Key with the verifying party
5. Key expires after one use or 30 days

### Custom Verification Letters

If you need a signed letter on company letterhead (for immigration, visa, bank, etc.):
1. Submit a request at HR Portal > Forms > Employment Verification
2. Specify what information to include:
   - Employment dates
   - Current title and department
   - Salary (optional — your consent required)
   - Employment status (full-time, part-time)
   - Custom language (if specific wording is required)
3. HR processes the request within 3 business days
4. Letter sent via email (PDF) or mailed (upon request)

### What HR Will NOT Provide

- Performance evaluations or ratings
- Reason for separation (for former employees)
- Personal opinions or recommendations
- Information not authorized by the employee

### Manager Reference Calls

- Managers may provide professional references when authorized by the employee
- References should include only factual, job-related information
- HR recommends limiting verbal references to confirming employment dates and title
- For anything beyond basic verification, direct the caller to HR

Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: Rehire eligibility and process — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

Can a former Engineering employee be rehired, and what is the process?

## Answer

The company welcomes back former employees ("boomerang hires") who left in good standing.

### Rehire Eligibility

| Separation Reason | Eligible for Rehire? |
|-------------------|---------------------|
| Voluntary resignation (good standing) | Yes |
| Layoff / Position elimination | Yes |
| Retirement | Yes |
| Mutual separation agreement | Case-by-case |
| Involuntary termination (performance) | After 12 months, case-by-case |
| Involuntary termination (misconduct) | Generally no |
| Job abandonment | Generally no |

### How to Check Your Status

- Former employees can email hr-records@company.com to confirm their rehire eligibility
- Hiring managers can check via the Recruiting Platform (former employee flag)

### Rehire Process

1. Former employee applies through the normal application process (jobs.company.com)
2. Recruiter verifies rehire eligibility in HRIS
3. Abbreviated interview process (hiring manager screen + 1 panel interview)
4. Background check required (even if done previously)
5. Standard offer process

### Benefits for Rehires

| Benefit | Policy |
|---------|--------|
| PTO accrual | Prior tenure credited if rehired within 12 months |
| 401(k) vesting | Prior vesting service credited |
| Seniority | Prior service may be credited for service awards |
| Benefits waiting period | Waived if rehired within 12 months |
| Salary | Based on current market and experience (not guaranteed to match previous salary) |
| Probationary period | Standard 90 days applies |

### Onboarding for Rehires

- Modified onboarding (abbreviated orientation — skip company overview)
- Full benefits enrollment within 30 days
- New I-9 required
- IT setup and systems access
- Team reintroduction and role-specific training

### Important Notes

- All rehire decisions require HR approval
- Former employees do not have priority over external candidates
- Non-compete or non-solicitation agreements from prior employment may still apply
- Start date must allow at least a 30-day gap from previous separation
Author: HR Compliance Team

| Involuntary termination (performance) | After 12 months, case-by-case |
| Involuntary termination (misconduct) | Generally no |
| Job abandonment | Generally no |

### How to Check Your Status

- Former employees can email hr-records@company.com to confirm their rehire eligibility
- Hiring managers can check via the Recruiting Platform (former employee flag)

### Rehire Process

1. Former employee applies through the normal application process (jobs.company.com)
2. Recruiter verifies rehire eligibility in HRIS
3. Abbreviated interview process (hiring manager screen + 1 panel interview)
4. Background check required (even if done previously)
5. Standard offer process

### Benefits for Rehires

| Benefit | Policy |
|---------|--------|
| PTO accrual | Prior tenure credited if rehired within 12 months |
| 401(k) vesting | Prior vesting service credited |
| Seniority | Prior service may be credited for service awards |
| Benefits waiting period | Waived if rehired within 12 months |
| Salary | Based on current market and experience (not guaranteed to match previous salary) |
| Probationary period | Standard 90 days applies |

### Onboarding for Rehires

- Modified onboarding (abbreviated orientation — skip company overview)
- Full benefits enrollment within 30 days
- New I-9 required
- IT setup and systems access
- Team reintroduction and role-specific training

### Important Notes

- All rehire decisions require HR approval
- Former employees do not have priority over external candidates
- Non-compete or non-solicitation agreements from prior employment may still apply
- Start date must allow at least a 30-day gap from previous separation

Author: Talent Acquisition

Title: How to write a self-assessment — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How should a Engineering employee approach writing their annual self-assessment?

## Answer

A well-written self-assessment helps your manager understand your contributions and supports accurate evaluation.

### Structure

Your self-assessment should address four areas:

**1. Goal Achievement (60% of assessment)**
For each goal set at the beginning of the year:
- State the goal
- Describe what you accomplished
- Provide specific metrics or evidence
- Note any obstacles and how you overcame them
- Self-rate your achievement (1-5)

**2. Key Accomplishments Beyond Goals (20%)**
- Projects or contributions not captured in formal goals
- Going above-and-beyond examples
- Impact on team, department, or company
- Awards, recognitions, or positive feedback received

**3. Development & Growth (10%)**
- Skills developed during the year
- Training completed, certifications earned
- Mentoring given or received
- Areas where you've grown

**4. Areas for Improvement (10%)**
- Honest reflection on challenges
- Skills you want to develop
- How you plan to improve
- Support or resources needed

### Writing Tips

**Do:**
- Use specific examples with measurable results
  - "Reduced onboarding time from 3 weeks to 1.5 weeks, saving 120 hours annually"
- Reference feedback you've received from others
- Be honest about both successes and challenges
- Connect your work to team and company goals
- Quantify wherever possible (%, $, hours, count)

**Don't:**
- Be vague: "I did a good job on the project"
- Only list activities without results
- Downplay your contributions (false modesty)
- Blame others for challenges
- Leave sections blank or write one-liners

### Sample Statements

**Weak**: "Worked on the customer portal project."
**Strong**: "Led the customer portal redesign, resulting in a 35% increase in self-service resolution rate and a 4.2/5 customer satisfaction score (up from 3.6). Delivered on time and $15K under budget."

### Timeline

- Self-assessment due: November 15
- Complete in the Performance Management Tool
- Allow 2-3 hours to write a thorough assessment
- Save drafts — you can revise until the deadline
Author: Employee Relations Team

- Awards, recognitions, or positive feedback received

**3. Development & Growth (10%)**
- Skills developed during the year
- Training completed, certifications earned
- Mentoring given or received
- Areas where you've grown

**4. Areas for Improvement (10%)**
- Honest reflection on challenges
- Skills you want to develop
- How you plan to improve
- Support or resources needed

### Writing Tips

**Do:**
- Use specific examples with measurable results
  - "Reduced onboarding time from 3 weeks to 1.5 weeks, saving 120 hours annually"
- Reference feedback you've received from others
- Be honest about both successes and challenges
- Connect your work to team and company goals
- Quantify wherever possible (%, $, hours, count)

**Don't:**
- Be vague: "I did a good job on the project"
- Only list activities without results
- Downplay your contributions (false modesty)
- Blame others for challenges
- Leave sections blank or write one-liners

### Sample Statements

**Weak**: "Worked on the customer portal project."
**Strong**: "Led the customer portal redesign, resulting in a 35% increase in self-service resolution rate and a 4.2/5 customer satisfaction score (up from 3.6). Delivered on time and $15K under budget."

### Timeline

- Self-assessment due: November 15
- Complete in the Performance Management Tool
- Allow 2-3 hours to write a thorough assessment
- Save drafts — you can revise until the deadline

Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: Employee resource groups overview — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) are available to Engineering employees?

## Answer

ERGs are employee-led groups that foster community, professional development, and cultural awareness.

### Active ERGs

| ERG | Focus | Open To |
|-----|-------|---------|
| Women@Company | Gender equity, women's advancement | All employees |
| Pride Network | LGBTQ+ community and allies | All employees |
| Black Professionals Network | Black employee community and development | All employees |
| LatinX Connect | Hispanic/Latinx community | All employees |
| Asian Pacific Alliance | Asian and Pacific Islander community | All employees |
| Veterans Network | Military veterans and supporters | All employees |
| Parents & Caregivers | Working parents and caregivers | All employees |
| disABILITY Alliance | Employees with disabilities and allies | All employees |
| Early Career Network | Employees in first 5 years of career | All employees |
| Interfaith Network | Religious and spiritual diversity | All employees |

### What ERGs Do

- **Community Building**: Social events, networking, cultural celebrations
- **Professional Development**: Mentoring, skill workshops, speaker events
- **Business Impact**: Advise on products, marketing, and policies from diverse perspectives
- **Recruiting**: Attend career fairs, review job postings, mentor new hires
- **Community Service**: Volunteer events aligned with the ERG's mission

### How to Join

1. Browse ERGs on the HR Portal > Diversity & Inclusion > ERGs
2. Click "Join" on any ERG page
3. You'll be added to the ERG's communication channel and event invitations
4. No limit on how many ERGs you can join
5. Membership is voluntary and confidential

### ERG Funding

- Each ERG receives an annual budget of $5,000-$15,000 (based on membership size)
- Funded activities: events, speakers, development programs, community donations
- Additional funding available through sponsorship requests to HR/DEI team

### Starting a New ERG

1. Gather at least 10 interested members
2. Identify an executive sponsor (VP+ level)
3. Submit a New ERG Proposal via the HR Portal
4. DEI team reviews and approves (within 30 days)
5. Receive startup funding and support

### Time Commitment

- ERG participation is encouraged during work hours
- Up to 4 hours/month of company time for ERG activities
- ERG leadership roles: up to 8 hours/month
- ERG contributions can be included in performance reviews under Cultural Goals

Title: Workplace accommodation for pregnancy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What workplace accommodations are available for pregnant Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company provides reasonable accommodations for pregnancy, childbirth, and related medical conditions.

### Your Rights

Under the Pregnant Workers Fairness Act (PWFA) and company policy:
- You are entitled to reasonable accommodations for pregnancy-related conditions
- You cannot be denied employment opportunities because of pregnancy
- You cannot be forced to take leave if a reasonable accommodation is available
- You are protected from retaliation for requesting accommodations

### Common Accommodations

| Accommodation | Examples |
|--------------|---------|
| Schedule modification | Later start time for morning sickness, more frequent breaks |
| Workspace adjustment | Closer to restroom, chair with lumbar support, standing desk |
| Workload adjustment | Temporary reduction in physical tasks, modified lifting requirements |
| Remote work | Additional remote days or temporary full remote |
| Dress code | Comfortable clothing, supportive footwear |
| Parking | Closer parking spot |
| Break time | Additional breaks for rest, snacks, or medical needs |
| Lactation | See lactation accommodation section below |

### How to Request

1. Notify your manager or HR that you need an accommodation
2. No formal paperwork required for simple accommodations
3. For more significant accommodations, submit a request via the HR Portal
4. HR may request a note from your healthcare provider (for specific restrictions)
5. Accommodation implemented typically within 5 business days

### Lactation Accommodations

Federal law (PUMP Act) requires:
- **Private space**: Dedicated lactation rooms on each floor (not a bathroom)
  - Rooms include: comfortable chair, table, outlet, sink, refrigerator, lock
- **Break time**: Reasonable break time to pump (typically 20-30 minutes, 2-3 times per day)
- **Duration**: For up to 1 year after childbirth
- **Flexibility**: Pump breaks are paid if taken during regular breaks; additional time may be unpaid for non-exempt employees

### Prenatal Appointments

- Use sick leave for prenatal medical appointments
- Flexible scheduling encouraged — work with your manager
- No penalty for attending necessary medical appointments

### Transition to Parental Leave

- Discuss your expected leave timing with your manager and HR at least 30 days before
- Create a workload transition plan
- HR coordinates FMLA, STD, and paid parental leave
- See the Parental Leave article for full details

### Contact

- HR Leave Team: hr-leave@company.com
- Employee Relations: employeerelations@company.com
Author: Employee Relations Team

5. Accommodation implemented typically within 5 business days

### Lactation Accommodations

Federal law (PUMP Act) requires:
- **Private space**: Dedicated lactation rooms on each floor (not a bathroom)
  - Rooms include: comfortable chair, table, outlet, sink, refrigerator, lock
- **Break time**: Reasonable break time to pump (typically 20-30 minutes, 2-3 times per day)
- **Duration**: For up to 1 year after childbirth
- **Flexibility**: Pump breaks are paid if taken during regular breaks; additional time may be unpaid for non-exempt employees

### Prenatal Appointments

- Use sick leave for prenatal medical appointments
- Flexible scheduling encouraged — work with your manager
- No penalty for attending necessary medical appointments

### Transition to Parental Leave

- Discuss your expected leave timing with your manager and HR at least 30 days before
- Create a workload transition plan
- HR coordinates FMLA, STD, and paid parental leave
- See the Parental Leave article for full details

### Contact

- HR Leave Team: hr-leave@company.com
- Employee Relations: employeerelations@company.com

Title: Acceptable use of company technology — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What is the acceptable use policy for company technology for Engineering employees?

## Answer

This policy covers the use of all company-provided technology resources.

### What's Covered

- Company-issued laptops, desktops, and mobile devices
- Company email and messaging systems
- Internet access through company networks
- Cloud services and SaaS applications
- Company phone systems
- Printers, scanners, and peripherals

### Acceptable Use

**You MAY:**
- Use company technology for authorized business purposes
- Make reasonable personal use during breaks (brief personal email, web browsing)
- Store business-related files on approved cloud services (OneDrive, Google Drive)
- Use personal devices to access company systems (per BYOD policy)
- Install approved software from the company software catalog

**You MAY NOT:**
- Access, download, or distribute illegal, offensive, or inappropriate content
- Use company technology for personal commercial ventures or side businesses
- Install unauthorized software or circumvent security controls
- Share your credentials with anyone (including IT support — they will never ask)
- Connect unauthorized devices to the company network
- Store company data on personal cloud services (personal Dropbox, Google Drive, etc.)
- Use company email for non-business mass communications
- Attempt to access systems or data beyond your authorization

### Monitoring

- The company reserves the right to monitor all use of company technology
- This includes: email, web browsing, file access, messaging, and phone usage
- Monitoring is for: security, compliance, and policy enforcement
- No expectation of privacy when using company systems
- Monitoring is conducted by IT Security, reviewed only for cause or audit

### Security Requirements

| Requirement | Details |
|------------|---------|
| Passwords | Minimum 12 characters, changed every 90 days, no reuse of last 10 |
| MFA | Required for all systems (authenticator app preferred) |
| Screen lock | Auto-lock after 5 minutes of inactivity |
| Encryption | Full-disk encryption enabled on all devices |
| VPN | Required when accessing company systems off-network |
| Updates | Install security patches within 48 hours of release |
| Public Wi-Fi | Never access sensitive company data on public Wi-Fi without VPN |

### Lost or Stolen Devices

1. Report immediately to IT Security: itsecurity@company.com or ext. 5200
2. IT can remotely wipe the device
3. File a police report if stolen
4. Complete an Incident Report via the HR Portal

### Violations

- First offense: Warning and remediation
- Repeat offenses: Escalating discipline up to termination
- Severe violations (data theft, sharing credentials, illegal activity): Immediate investigation and potential termination
Author: Learning & Development

- Monitoring is conducted by IT Security, reviewed only for cause or audit

### Security Requirements

| Requirement | Details |
|------------|---------|
| Passwords | Minimum 12 characters, changed every 90 days, no reuse of last 10 |
| MFA | Required for all systems (authenticator app preferred) |
| Screen lock | Auto-lock after 5 minutes of inactivity |
| Encryption | Full-disk encryption enabled on all devices |
| VPN | Required when accessing company systems off-network |
| Updates | Install security patches within 48 hours of release |
| Public Wi-Fi | Never access sensitive company data on public Wi-Fi without VPN |

### Lost or Stolen Devices

1. Report immediately to IT Security: itsecurity@company.com or ext. 5200
2. IT can remotely wipe the device
3. File a police report if stolen
4. Complete an Incident Report via the HR Portal

### Violations

- First offense: Warning and remediation
- Repeat offenses: Escalating discipline up to termination
- Severe violations (data theft, sharing credentials, illegal activity): Immediate investigation and potential termination
Author: HR Generalist

Title: Conflict of interest disclosure — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

When and how should Engineering employees disclose potential conflicts of interest?

## Answer

All employees must disclose actual or potential conflicts of interest to protect both the employee and the company.

### What Is a Conflict of Interest

A conflict exists when your personal interests or activities could influence, or appear to influence, your professional judgment or actions.

### Common Examples

| Situation | Conflict? |
|-----------|----------|
| You own stock in a vendor the company uses | Potential conflict — disclose |
| Your spouse works for a competitor | Potential conflict — disclose |
| You have a side consulting business in the same industry | Likely conflict — disclose |
| You serve on a nonprofit board | Usually fine — disclose if overlaps |
| You want to hire a friend or family member | Conflict — disclose and recuse from hiring |
| A vendor offers you expensive gifts | Conflict — decline or report |
| You invest in a company your team evaluates | Conflict — disclose and recuse |

### Annual Disclosure Requirement

1. All employees complete the Annual Conflict of Interest Disclosure by February 28
2. Available on the HR Portal > Compliance > COI Disclosure
3. Disclose all:
   - Outside employment or consulting
   - Board memberships (for-profit and nonprofit)
   - Financial interests in vendors, clients, or competitors (over $10,000 or 1% ownership)
   - Family members employed by vendors, clients, or competitors
   - Gifts received valued over $100
4. If nothing to disclose, submit a "No Conflicts" attestation

### Ongoing Disclosure

- Report new conflicts as they arise (don't wait for annual disclosure)
- Email: ethics@company.com or use the COI Disclosure form
- When in doubt, disclose — it's always better to ask

### Review Process

1. Compliance team reviews all disclosures within 15 business days
2. Possible outcomes:
   - **No action needed**: Noted and filed
   - **Management plan**: Recusal from certain decisions, information barriers
   - **Modification required**: Divest interest, resign board seat, end outside employment
   - **Prohibition**: If conflict cannot be managed
3. You are notified of the determination in writing

### Gifts and Entertainment

| Item | Permitted? |
|------|-----------|
| Gifts under $100 | Yes (occasional, non-cash) |
| Gifts over $100 | No — decline and notify manager |
| Business meals | Yes (reasonable, infrequent, documented) |
| Entertainment (events, concerts) | Under $250 with manager knowledge |
| Cash or cash equivalents | Never permitted |
| Gifts from government officials | Never permitted |
| Gifts to government officials | Never permitted (anti-corruption policy) |

- Email: ethics@company.com or use the COI Disclosure form
- When in doubt, disclose — it's always better to ask

### Review Process

1. Compliance team reviews all disclosures within 15 business days
2. Possible outcomes:
   - **No action needed**: Noted and filed
   - **Management plan**: Recusal from certain decisions, information barriers
   - **Modification required**: Divest interest, resign board seat, end outside employment
   - **Prohibition**: If conflict cannot be managed
3. You are notified of the determination in writing

### Gifts and Entertainment

| Item | Permitted? |
|------|-----------|
| Gifts under $100 | Yes (occasional, non-cash) |
| Gifts over $100 | No — decline and notify manager |
| Business meals | Yes (reasonable, infrequent, documented) |
| Entertainment (events, concerts) | Under $250 with manager knowledge |
| Cash or cash equivalents | Never permitted |
| Gifts from government officials | Never permitted |
| Gifts to government officials | Never permitted (anti-corruption policy) |

Title: Conflict of interest disclosure — Procurement department
Content:
## Question

When and how should Procurement employees disclose potential conflicts of interest?

## Answer

All employees must disclose actual or potential conflicts of interest to protect both the employee and the company.

### What Is a Conflict of Interest

A conflict exists when your personal interests or activities could influence, or appear to influence, your professional judgment or actions.

### Common Examples

| Situation | Conflict? |
|-----------|----------|
| You own stock in a vendor the company uses | Potential conflict — disclose |
| Your spouse works for a competitor | Potential conflict — disclose |
| You have a side consulting business in the same industry | Likely conflict — disclose |
| You serve on a nonprofit board | Usually fine — disclose if overlaps |
| You want to hire a friend or family member | Conflict — disclose and recuse from hiring |
| A vendor offers you expensive gifts | Conflict — decline or report |
| You invest in a company your team evaluates | Conflict — disclose and recuse |

### Annual Disclosure Requirement

1. All employees complete the Annual Conflict of Interest Disclosure by February 28
2. Available on the HR Portal > Compliance > COI Disclosure
3. Disclose all:
   - Outside employment or consulting
   - Board memberships (for-profit and nonprofit)
   - Financial interests in vendors, clients, or competitors (over $10,000 or 1% ownership)
   - Family members employed by vendors, clients, or competitors
   - Gifts received valued over $100
4. If nothing to disclose, submit a "No Conflicts" attestation

### Ongoing Disclosure

- Report new conflicts as they arise (don't wait for annual disclosure)
- Email: ethics@company.com or use the COI Disclosure form
- When in doubt, disclose — it's always better to ask

### Review Process

1. Compliance team reviews all disclosures within 15 business days
2. Possible outcomes:
   - **No action needed**: Noted and filed
   - **Management plan**: Recusal from certain decisions, information barriers
   - **Modification required**: Divest interest, resign board seat, end outside employment
   - **Prohibition**: If conflict cannot be managed
3. You are notified of the determination in writing

| Item | Permitted? |
|------|-----------|
| Gifts under $100 | Yes (occasional, non-cash) |
| Gifts over $100 | No — decline and notify manager |
| Business meals | Yes (reasonable, infrequent, documented) |

Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: How payroll handles retroactive pay adjustments — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How are retroactive pay adjustments handled for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Retroactive adjustments correct pay for past periods when changes were approved with an effective date in the past.

### Common Retroactive Scenarios

| Scenario | Example |
|----------|---------|
| Late promotion processing | Promoted effective April 1, processed in May |
| Merit increase delay | Annual increases approved in March, effective January 1 |
| Reclassification | Job reclassified with retroactive effective date |
| Payroll error correction | Incorrect rate applied to one or more pay periods |
| Overtime miscalculation | Missed overtime hours identified |

### How It Works

1. **Identification**: Manager, HR, or Payroll identifies the need for adjustment
2. **Calculation**: Payroll calculates the difference between what was paid and what should have been paid
3. **Approval**: Manager and HR approve the adjustment
4. **Processing**: Adjustment included in the next regular paycheck
5. **Documentation**: Line item on pay stub shows "Retro Adjustment" with dates

### Tax Implications

- Retroactive pay is taxed in the pay period it is received (not the period it was earned)
- This may temporarily increase your tax withholding for that pay period
- Year-end W-2 reflects total actual compensation paid during the calendar year
- If retro pay crosses calendar years, special handling may be required (consult Payroll)

### Timeline

| Adjustment Type | Processing Time |
|----------------|----------------|
| Simple rate correction | Next pay period |
| Merit/promotion retro | 1-2 pay periods |
| Complex multi-period | 2-3 pay periods |
| Cross-calendar-year | Case-by-case (may require W-2c) |

### If You Believe You Were Underpaid

1. Review your pay stubs on ess.company.com
2. Compare actual pay to your offer letter, promotion letter, or merit notification
3. If discrepancy exists, email payroll@company.com with:
   - Your name and employee ID
   - Pay periods affected
   - Expected vs. actual pay
   - Supporting documentation (offer letter, etc.)
4. Payroll investigates and responds within 5 business days

### Overpayment Recovery

If you were overpaid:
- Payroll will notify you in writing
- Repayment plan arranged (typically spread over multiple pay periods)
- Will not exceed 10% of gross pay per pay period
- You will receive full documentation of the overpayment and recovery plan
- Deductions stop if you dispute the overpayment (pending resolution)

Title: How payroll handles retroactive pay adjustments — Executive department
Content:
## Question

How are retroactive pay adjustments handled for Executive employees?

## Answer

Retroactive adjustments correct pay for past periods when changes were approved with an effective date in the past.

### Common Retroactive Scenarios

| Scenario | Example |
|----------|---------|
| Late promotion processing | Promoted effective April 1, processed in May |
| Merit increase delay | Annual increases approved in March, effective January 1 |
| Reclassification | Job reclassified with retroactive effective date |
| Payroll error correction | Incorrect rate applied to one or more pay periods |
| Overtime miscalculation | Missed overtime hours identified |

### How It Works

1. **Identification**: Manager, HR, or Payroll identifies the need for adjustment
2. **Calculation**: Payroll calculates the difference between what was paid and what should have been paid
3. **Approval**: Manager and HR approve the adjustment
4. **Processing**: Adjustment included in the next regular paycheck
5. **Documentation**: Line item on pay stub shows "Retro Adjustment" with dates

### Tax Implications

- Retroactive pay is taxed in the pay period it is received (not the period it was earned)
- This may temporarily increase your tax withholding for that pay period
- Year-end W-2 reflects total actual compensation paid during the calendar year
- If retro pay crosses calendar years, special handling may be required (consult Payroll)

### Timeline

| Adjustment Type | Processing Time |
|----------------|----------------|
| Simple rate correction | Next pay period |
| Merit/promotion retro | 1-2 pay periods |
| Complex multi-period | 2-3 pay periods |
| Cross-calendar-year | Case-by-case (may require W-2c) |

### If You Believe You Were Underpaid

1. Review your pay stubs on ess.company.com
2. Compare actual pay to your offer letter, promotion letter, or merit notification
3. If discrepancy exists, email payroll@company.com with:
   - Your name and employee ID
   - Pay periods affected
   - Expected vs. actual pay
   - Supporting documentation (offer letter, etc.)
4. Payroll investigates and responds within 5 business days

### Overpayment Recovery

If you were overpaid:
- Payroll will notify you in writing
- Repayment plan arranged (typically spread over multiple pay periods)
- Will not exceed 10% of gross pay per pay period
- You will receive full documentation of the overpayment and recovery plan
- Deductions stop if you dispute the overpayment (pending resolution)

Author: Payroll Team

Title: International payroll and multi-state tax — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does payroll handle multi-state or international work for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Working from different states or countries creates tax obligations that Payroll must manage.

### Multi-State Employees

If you work in a state different from your home state or office location:

**Permanent Remote in Different State**
- Payroll withholds taxes for your work state (where you physically perform work)
- Some states also tax you based on employer location (convenience rule states: NY, NJ, PA, CT, DE, NE)
- You may need to file returns in both states
- Credits typically prevent double taxation

**Temporary Travel to Other States**
- If you travel for business to other states, some states require withholding after a threshold (varies: 1 day to 30 days)
- Payroll tracks business travel and applies state rules
- Log all work travel in the Travel Calendar (HR Portal > Payroll > Travel Calendar)

### What You Need to Do

1. **Notify HR/Payroll** before changing your work location (even temporarily for 30+ days)
2. **Complete state tax withholding forms** for each work state
3. **Log business travel** in the Travel Calendar monthly
4. **File tax returns** in all required states (company provides a stipend for additional tax prep — see below)

### Multi-State Tax Preparation Stipend

- Employees working in 2+ states receive a $200 annual tax preparation stipend
- Reimbursed through the expense system
- Intended to cover the additional cost of multi-state tax filing

### International Employees / Travel

| Situation | Handling |
|-----------|---------|
| US employee traveling internationally | Report trips to Payroll; tax treaty analysis may apply |
| US employee temporarily working abroad (< 183 days) | May still owe taxes in host country |
| US employee on international assignment | Global mobility team manages; shadow payroll may apply |
| Non-US employee working in US | Visa and tax status determine withholding |

### Important: Moving to a New State

If you plan to relocate:
1. Notify HR at least 30 days before the move
2. HR/Legal verifies the company can legally employ in that state
3. Payroll updates tax withholding
4. Benefits may be affected (health plan network changes)
5. Employment laws of the new state apply (which may change your leave, overtime, or other rights)

### Contact

- Payroll: payroll@company.com
- Global Mobility: globalmobility@company.com
- Tax questions: Consult a personal tax professional (company does not provide individual tax advice)

Title: International payroll and multi-state tax — Product department
Content:
## Question

How does payroll handle multi-state or international work for Product employees?

## Answer

Working from different states or countries creates tax obligations that Payroll must manage.

### Multi-State Employees

If you work in a state different from your home state or office location:

**Permanent Remote in Different State**
- Payroll withholds taxes for your work state (where you physically perform work)
- Some states also tax you based on employer location (convenience rule states: NY, NJ, PA, CT, DE, NE)
- You may need to file returns in both states
- Credits typically prevent double taxation

**Temporary Travel to Other States**
- If you travel for business to other states, some states require withholding after a threshold (varies: 1 day to 30 days)
- Payroll tracks business travel and applies state rules
- Log all work travel in the Travel Calendar (HR Portal > Payroll > Travel Calendar)

### What You Need to Do

1. **Notify HR/Payroll** before changing your work location (even temporarily for 30+ days)
2. **Complete state tax withholding forms** for each work state
3. **Log business travel** in the Travel Calendar monthly
4. **File tax returns** in all required states (company provides a stipend for additional tax prep — see below)

### Multi-State Tax Preparation Stipend

- Employees working in 2+ states receive a $200 annual tax preparation stipend
- Reimbursed through the expense system
- Intended to cover the additional cost of multi-state tax filing

### International Employees / Travel

| Situation | Handling |
|-----------|---------|
| US employee traveling internationally | Report trips to Payroll; tax treaty analysis may apply |
| US employee temporarily working abroad (< 183 days) | May still owe taxes in host country |
| US employee on international assignment | Global mobility team manages; shadow payroll may apply |
| Non-US employee working in US | Visa and tax status determine withholding |

### Important: Moving to a New State

If you plan to relocate:
1. Notify HR at least 30 days before the move
2. HR/Legal verifies the company can legally employ in that state
3. Payroll updates tax withholding
4. Benefits may be affected (health plan network changes)
5. Employment laws of the new state apply (which may change your leave, overtime, or other rights)

Author: Talent Acquisition

Title: Cross-functional rotation programs — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How do cross-functional rotation programs work for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Rotation programs allow employees to gain experience in different departments to broaden skills and perspective.

### Program Types

| Program | Who | Duration | Time Commitment |
|---------|-----|----------|-----------------|
| Shadow Program | All employees | 1-2 days | Full days |
| Mini Rotation | L2-L4 | 4-6 weeks | 50% of time |
| Full Rotation | High-potential L3-L5 | 6 months | 100% |
| Executive Rotation | L6+ | 12 months | 100% |

### Shadow Program (1-2 Days)

- Spend 1-2 days observing another team's work
- Open to all employees with manager approval
- Great for exploring career interests or understanding cross-functional partners
- Request via the LMS > "Job Shadow Request"
- No formal application — coordinate with the host team

### Mini Rotation (4-6 Weeks)

- Split time between current role (50%) and rotation role (50%)
- Work on a specific project with the rotation team
- Current manager and rotation manager co-manage
- No compensation change
- Application:
  1. Discuss interest with your manager
  2. Submit a Mini Rotation Request on the HR Portal
  3. L&D matches you with an available opportunity
  4. Both managers agree on timing and deliverables

### Full Rotation (6 Months)

- Fully embedded in the new team for 6 months
- Take on a substantive role with real responsibilities
- Your original position is held for your return
- Selected through the Talent Review process (high-potential identification)
- Nomination by your manager + VP approval
- 2 cohorts per year (starting January and July)

### Executive Rotation (12 Months)

- Senior leaders rotate through a different business unit or function
- Designed to build enterprise-wide leadership capability
- Selected by the executive team during succession planning
- Compensation and level remain the same
- Often a prerequisite for SVP/C-level advancement

### Benefits of Rotations

- Build broader skill set and business acumen
- Expand internal network and collaboration
- Discover new career interests
- Bring fresh perspectives to both teams
- Rotation experience valued in promotion decisions

### After Your Rotation

- Return to your original team (or negotiate a permanent move if both sides agree)
- Debrief with your manager on what you learned
- Apply new skills and perspectives to your role
- Share insights with your team
- Include rotation experience in your performance review
Author: HR Benefits Team

  4. Both managers agree on timing and deliverables

### Full Rotation (6 Months)

- Take on a substantive role with real responsibilities
- Your original position is held for your return
- Selected through the Talent Review process (high-potential identification)
- Nomination by your manager + VP approval
- 2 cohorts per year (starting January and July)

### Executive Rotation (12 Months)

- Senior leaders rotate through a different business unit or function
- Designed to build enterprise-wide leadership capability
- Selected by the executive team during succession planning
- Compensation and level remain the same
- Often a prerequisite for SVP/C-level advancement

### Benefits of Rotations

- Build broader skill set and business acumen
- Expand internal network and collaboration
- Discover new career interests
- Bring fresh perspectives to both teams
- Rotation experience valued in promotion decisions

### After Your Rotation

- Return to your original team (or negotiate a permanent move if both sides agree)
- Debrief with your manager on what you learned
- Apply new skills and perspectives to your role
- Share insights with your team
- Include rotation experience in your performance review

Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: Coaching and executive coaching — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What coaching resources are available for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company offers multiple tiers of coaching to support employee development.

### Coaching Programs

**Peer Coaching Circles (All Employees)**
- Small groups of 4-6 employees who meet bi-weekly
- Facilitated discussion on challenges and goals
- Self-organized or through the LMS
- No cost, company time approved (2 hours/month)

**Internal Coaching (L3+ Employees)**
- 6-session engagement with a trained internal coach
- Coaches are certified employees from other departments (ICF-trained)
- Topics: career development, leadership skills, communication, transition support
- Request via LMS > "Coaching Request"
- Available year-round, matched within 2 weeks

**External Professional Coaching (L5+ Managers)**
- 12-session engagement with an ICF-certified external coach
- Typical engagement: 6-12 months
- Company covers up to $5,000 per engagement
- For developing leadership skills, navigating organizational challenges, executive presence
- Request through your manager and HR

**Executive Coaching (L7+ / VP and above)**
- Premium coaching with C-suite experienced coaches
- 12-month engagement, bi-weekly sessions
- Company covers up to $25,000 per engagement
- Includes 360 feedback, personality assessments, and development plan
- Arranged by HR in partnership with the executive

### How Coaching Differs from Mentoring and Therapy

| Aspect | Coaching | Mentoring | Therapy |
|--------|---------|-----------|---------|
| Focus | Performance and goals | Career guidance | Emotional wellbeing |
| Duration | Defined engagement | Ongoing relationship | As needed |
| Who | Trained coach | Experienced colleague | Licensed therapist |
| Direction | Coach asks questions, client finds answers | Mentor shares advice | Therapist treats |
| Confidential | Yes (within reason) | Generally yes | Legally protected |

### Getting the Most from Coaching

- Come prepared with specific topics for each session
- Be open and honest about challenges
- Commit to action items between sessions
- Practice new skills immediately
- Give feedback to your coach about what's working
- Share progress with your manager (at your comfort level)

### Contact

- L&D Team: learning@company.com
- Coaching Program Manager: coaching@company.com
Author: Talent Acquisition

- Company covers up to $5,000 per engagement
- For developing leadership skills, navigating organizational challenges, executive presence
- Request through your manager and HR

**Executive Coaching (L7+ / VP and above)**

| Aspect | Coaching | Mentoring | Therapy |

- Come prepared with specific topics for each session
- Be open and honest about challenges
- Commit to action items between sessions
- Practice new skills immediately
- Give feedback to your coach about what's working

Title: Chemical safety and hazard communication — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What do Engineering employees need to know about chemical safety and hazard communication?

## Answer

The company complies with OSHA's Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) to ensure employee safety around chemicals.

### Right to Know

All employees have the right to know about hazardous chemicals in their workplace. This includes:
- What chemicals are present
- What hazards they pose
- How to protect yourself
- What to do in case of exposure

### Safety Data Sheets (SDS)

- SDS are available for every hazardous chemical on-site
- **Location**: Binders in each department, plus online at safety.company.com > SDS Library
- SDS contain 16 sections including: identification, hazards, first aid, handling, exposure controls

### GHS Labels

All chemical containers must have a GHS-compliant label with:
- Product identifier (name)
- Signal word (Danger or Warning)
- Hazard pictograms (flame, skull, exclamation mark, etc.)
- Hazard statements (describes the hazard)
- Precautionary statements (prevention, response, storage, disposal)
- Supplier information

### Common Workplace Chemicals

| Chemical | Location | Hazard | Protection |
|----------|---------|--------|-----------|
| Toner (printers) | All floors | Dust inhalation | Ventilation, gloves for changes |
| Cleaning products | Janitorial closets | Skin/eye irritation | Gloves, goggles |
| Hand sanitizer | All areas | Flammable | Keep from heat/flames |
| Battery acid (UPS) | Server room | Corrosive | Trained personnel only |
| Compressed gas | HVAC, labs | Pressure, asphyxiation | Trained personnel only |

### Training

- **Initial training**: During new hire orientation (all employees)
- **Annual refresher**: Required HazCom training via LMS (30 minutes)
- **Job-specific**: Additional training for employees who handle chemicals regularly
- **Before new chemicals**: Training before introducing new chemicals to the workplace

### In Case of Exposure

1. Remove yourself from the area
2. Follow first aid instructions on the SDS/label
3. For skin/eye contact: Use the nearest eyewash station or safety shower (15 minutes)
4. Call 911 for serious exposures
5. Report to your supervisor and EHS (ext. 5600)
6. Seek medical attention even for minor exposures (document everything)

### Eyewash and Safety Shower Locations

- Each floor near the janitorial area

Author: Payroll Team

Title: Chemical safety and hazard communication — Sales department
Content:
## Question

What do Sales employees need to know about chemical safety and hazard communication?

## Answer

The company complies with OSHA's Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) to ensure employee safety around chemicals.

### Right to Know

All employees have the right to know about hazardous chemicals in their workplace. This includes:
- What chemicals are present
- What hazards they pose
- How to protect yourself
- What to do in case of exposure

### Safety Data Sheets (SDS)

- SDS are available for every hazardous chemical on-site
- **Location**: Binders in each department, plus online at safety.company.com > SDS Library
- SDS contain 16 sections including: identification, hazards, first aid, handling, exposure controls

### GHS Labels

All chemical containers must have a GHS-compliant label with:
- Product identifier (name)
- Signal word (Danger or Warning)
- Hazard pictograms (flame, skull, exclamation mark, etc.)
- Hazard statements (describes the hazard)
- Precautionary statements (prevention, response, storage, disposal)
- Supplier information

### Common Workplace Chemicals

| Chemical | Location | Hazard | Protection |
|----------|---------|--------|-----------|
| Toner (printers) | All floors | Dust inhalation | Ventilation, gloves for changes |
| Cleaning products | Janitorial closets | Skin/eye irritation | Gloves, goggles |
| Hand sanitizer | All areas | Flammable | Keep from heat/flames |
| Battery acid (UPS) | Server room | Corrosive | Trained personnel only |
| Compressed gas | HVAC, labs | Pressure, asphyxiation | Trained personnel only |

### Training

- **Initial training**: During new hire orientation (all employees)
- **Annual refresher**: Required HazCom training via LMS (30 minutes)
- **Job-specific**: Additional training for employees who handle chemicals regularly
- **Before new chemicals**: Training before introducing new chemicals to the workplace

### In Case of Exposure

1. Remove yourself from the area
2. Follow first aid instructions on the SDS/label
3. For skin/eye contact: Use the nearest eyewash station or safety shower (15 minutes)
4. Call 911 for serious exposures
5. Report to your supervisor and EHS (ext. 5600)
6. Seek medical attention even for minor exposures (document everything)

### Eyewash and Safety Shower Locations

- Each floor near the janitorial area
- Labs and chemical storage areas
- Loading dock
- Inspected monthly by Facilities (inspection tags visible)
Author: Payroll Team

- Hazard statements (describes the hazard)
- Precautionary statements (prevention, response, storage, disposal)
- Supplier information

### Common Workplace Chemicals

| Chemical | Location | Hazard | Protection |
|----------|---------|--------|-----------|
| Toner (printers) | All floors | Dust inhalation | Ventilation, gloves for changes |
| Cleaning products | Janitorial closets | Skin/eye irritation | Gloves, goggles |
| Hand sanitizer | All areas | Flammable | Keep from heat/flames |
| Battery acid (UPS) | Server room | Corrosive | Trained personnel only |
| Compressed gas | HVAC, labs | Pressure, asphyxiation | Trained personnel only |

### Training

- **Initial training**: During new hire orientation (all employees)
- **Annual refresher**: Required HazCom training via LMS (30 minutes)
- **Job-specific**: Additional training for employees who handle chemicals regularly
- **Before new chemicals**: Training before introducing new chemicals to the workplace

### In Case of Exposure

1. Remove yourself from the area
2. Follow first aid instructions on the SDS/label
3. For skin/eye contact: Use the nearest eyewash station or safety shower (15 minutes)
4. Call 911 for serious exposures
5. Report to your supervisor and EHS (ext. 5600)
6. Seek medical attention even for minor exposures (document everything)

### Eyewash and Safety Shower Locations

- Each floor near the janitorial area
- Labs and chemical storage areas
- Loading dock
- Inspected monthly by Facilities (inspection tags visible)
Author: Talent Acquisition

Title: Chemical safety and hazard communication — Administration department
Content:
## Question

What do Administration employees need to know about chemical safety and hazard communication?

## Answer

The company complies with OSHA's Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) to ensure employee safety around chemicals.

### Right to Know

All employees have the right to know about hazardous chemicals in their workplace. This includes:
- What chemicals are present
- What hazards they pose
- How to protect yourself
- What to do in case of exposure

### Safety Data Sheets (SDS)

- SDS are available for every hazardous chemical on-site
- **Location**: Binders in each department, plus online at safety.company.com > SDS Library
- SDS contain 16 sections including: identification, hazards, first aid, handling, exposure controls

### GHS Labels

All chemical containers must have a GHS-compliant label with:
- Product identifier (name)
- Signal word (Danger or Warning)
- Hazard pictograms (flame, skull, exclamation mark, etc.)
- Hazard statements (describes the hazard)
- Precautionary statements (prevention, response, storage, disposal)
- Supplier information

### Common Workplace Chemicals

| Chemical | Location | Hazard | Protection |
|----------|---------|--------|-----------|
| Toner (printers) | All floors | Dust inhalation | Ventilation, gloves for changes |
| Cleaning products | Janitorial closets | Skin/eye irritation | Gloves, goggles |
| Hand sanitizer | All areas | Flammable | Keep from heat/flames |
| Battery acid (UPS) | Server room | Corrosive | Trained personnel only |
| Compressed gas | HVAC, labs | Pressure, asphyxiation | Trained personnel only |

### Training

- **Initial training**: During new hire orientation (all employees)
- **Annual refresher**: Required HazCom training via LMS (30 minutes)
- **Job-specific**: Additional training for employees who handle chemicals regularly
- **Before new chemicals**: Training before introducing new chemicals to the workplace

### In Case of Exposure

1. Remove yourself from the area
2. Follow first aid instructions on the SDS/label
3. For skin/eye contact: Use the nearest eyewash station or safety shower (15 minutes)
4. Call 911 for serious exposures
5. Report to your supervisor and EHS (ext. 5600)
6. Seek medical attention even for minor exposures (document everything)

### Eyewash and Safety Shower Locations

- Each floor near the janitorial area
- Labs and chemical storage areas
- Loading dock
- Inspected monthly by Facilities (inspection tags visible)
Author: HR Operations

Title: Relocation assistance for new hires — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What relocation assistance is available for Engineering new hires or transferring employees?

## Answer

The company provides relocation benefits for employees who need to move for their role.

### Eligibility

- New hires relocating 50+ miles for the position
- Current employees transferring to a new office location 50+ miles away
- Must be a full-time, salaried position at L3 or above
- Relocation must be approved by the hiring manager and HR

### Relocation Packages

| Level | Package Value | Lump Sum Option |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|
| L3-L4 | Up to $10,000 | $7,500 lump sum |
| L5-L6 | Up to $25,000 | $18,000 lump sum |
| L7+ | Up to $50,000 | Custom |

### What's Covered (Managed Package)

| Expense | Coverage |
|---------|---------|
| House-hunting trip | 2 trips, up to 3 days each (flights, hotel, rental car) |
| Moving company | Full-service pack, transport, and unpack |
| Temporary housing | Up to 60 days |
| Storage | Up to 60 days |
| Final move travel | Employee + family travel to new location |
| Lease break assistance | Up to 2 months' rent |
| Real estate assistance | Closing costs up to $5,000 (homeowner) |
| Miscellaneous | $2,000 for incidentals |

### Lump Sum Option

- Receive a single payment instead of managed relocation
- More flexibility — you manage your own move
- Paid in the first paycheck (taxes withheld)
- No receipts or expense reports required

### Tax Gross-Up

- Relocation benefits are taxable income (per IRS rules)
- The company provides a tax gross-up on managed relocation benefits
- Lump sum: No gross-up (amount is the net after standard withholding)

### Repayment Agreement

| Departure Timing | Repayment |
|-----------------|-----------|
| Within 12 months | 100% of relocation costs |
| 12-24 months | 50% of relocation costs |
| After 24 months | No repayment |

### Process

1. Hiring manager includes relocation in the offer approval
2. HR sends relocation package details with the offer letter
3. Employee selects managed package or lump sum
4. For managed package: Company relocation vendor contacts employee within 5 business days
5. Submit all expenses per the relocation timeline (within 12 months of start date)

### Contact

- Relocation Coordinator: relocation@company.com
- Relocation Vendor: 800-555-0444
Author: Payroll Team
